title: The Ultimate Solopreneur Agent Harness for AI Builders (Claude Code Harness)
summary: Ron Demerit built a "Solopreneur Rig" harness for Claude Code with 16 specialized sub-agents, a product manager orchestrator, and session locking for isolated client work. The harness enables dynamic model routing between Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku, demonstrated through three applications: Spanish-learning music player, local macOS Whisper transcription, and Hermit Host self-hosted deployment platform.
writeup: Ron Demerit walks through his Claude Code solopreneur harness, a structured development rig built on top of Scott Graham's initial templates, and demos two apps it produced: Canta Con Migo, a Spanish music learning app with word-level definitions and AI-generated annotations, and a toolchain that drives the full content pipeline for that app. The through-line is that a well-designed Claude Code harness generates whole project repositories with typed slash commands, manages context window size via a CTX plugin, and handles tasks like lyric extraction, translation, Whisper-based audio alignment, and SQL ingestion, turning a single developer into a functionally complete team.
key_points: The solopreneur harness is built on top of Claude Code and generates a full project repository, including front end, back end, SQL database schema, and a set of slash commands for post-processing tasks, so each new project starts from a consistent, compartmentalized scaffold rather than a blank vibe-code session.

Canta Con Migo processes Spanish music videos offline using Claude for translation and annotation, a local Whisper instance for lyric alignment, and a SQL database as the backend, then serves the result as a static site where lyrics highlight in sync and words pop up with definitions on click.

A CTX plugin keeps Claude's context window small by offloading intermediate processing results to a local database and returning only the final output to the LLM, which reduces token usage and extends practical session length without losing Claude's awareness of the task outcome.

Lyric timing alignment uses a two-pass approach: Whisper attempts automatic alignment first, generating a YAML file; if the result is off, a custom browser-based calibration UI lets the operator scrub the video with keyboard shortcuts and press Space to re-mark each lyric line, matching the workflow of professional subtitling tools.

The harness paradigm separates vibe-coding (rapid generation of application code) from structured slash-command workflows (repeatable, parameterized operations like song import, translate, and align), so the generated projects are maintainable and extensible rather than one-shot scripts.
